prompt: A photorealistic cinematic top-down drone shot of trees arranged in four clusters connected by branching trunks and roots, forming a growing network from left to right. The first cluster has 1 tree, the second cluster has 3 trees, the third cluster has 6 trees, and the fourth cluster has 10 trees. Within each cluster the trees are interconnected by visible woody branches forming small lattice-like networks, while a thicker main branch connects each cluster sequentially.The scene is viewed from a perfectly vertical bird’s-eye perspective (90° drone POV). Each tree has dense green foliage and realistic trunks casting long natural shadows.The ground is not a flat lawn: it is a richly detailed natural terrain with uneven grassy meadows, subtle elevation changes, gentle bumps and shallow dips, patches of darker and lighter grass, areas of exposed soil, scattered weeds, small rocks, and natural texture variation. The terrain shows slight slopes and organic irregularities, creating a believable landscape rather than a uniform plane.Lighting is warm natural sunlight with cinematic HDR, producing soft shadows and highlighting the terrain contours. Ultra-realistic textures, global illumination, physically accurate lighting, high environmental detail, 8k photorealistic render.
visual_logic: Each group grows larger from left to right. A tree in each group holds the branch of tree in the next, larger group, visually showing how your friends tend to have bigger friend circles.
